publications.title: Specificity of bispecific T cell receptors and antibodies targeting peptide-HLA.
publications.authors: Holland CJ, Crean RM, Pentier JM, de Wet B, Lloyd A, Srikannathasan V, Lissin N, Lloyd KA, Blicher TH, Conroy PJ, Hock M, Pengelly RJ, Spinner TE, Cameron B, Potter EA, Jeyanthan A, Molloy PE, Sami M, Aleksic M, Liddy N, Robinson RA, Harper S, Lepore M, Pudney CR, van der Kamp MW, Rizkallah PJ, Jakobsen BK, Vuidepot A, Cole DK.
publications.executive_summary: This study compared the specificity and molecular binding mechanisms of two classes of bispecific T cell-redirecting agents targeting peptide–HLA complexes on cancer cells: affinity-enhanced natural TCRs (formatted as ImmTAC molecules) and TCR-mimic antibodies. Using structural, biochemical, and computational analysis, the authors found that enhanced TCRs distribute binding interactions broadly across multiple HLA and peptide residues, resulting in greater target selectivity and reduced off-target engagement, whereas TCR-mimic antibodies concentrate binding on HLA "hot spots," leading to increased cross-reactivity across different pHLA variants. These findings address a critical challenge in cancer immunotherapy—avoiding off-target activation against the enormous repertoire of pHLA presented on healthy cells—and establish molecular principles to guide the rational design of future pHLA-targeting bispecifics for safer and more specific adoptive T cell therapies.
